front: What is the primary function of the Network Layer (Layer 3) in the OSI model?
back: Its main function is logical addressing and routing. It determines the path data packets take from the source to the destination host across multiple networks.

front: Differentiate between a router and a switch.
back: A switch operates at Layer 2 (Data Link) and forwards frames within a single network based on MAC addresses. A router operates at Layer 3 (Network) and routes packets between different networks based on IP addresses.

front: What is the purpose of the subnet mask in an IP address?
back: The subnet mask distinguishes the network portion from the host portion of an IP address. It is used by devices to determine if a destination host is on the same local network or a remote one.

front: Define the term 'Routing Protocol' and name two common examples.
back: A routing protocol is a set of rules used by routers to communicate and share information about network topology, enabling them to select the best paths. Examples: OSPF (Open Shortest Path First) and BGP (Border Gateway Protocol).

front: What is the core difference between IPv4 and IPv6?
back: The most significant difference is the address space. IPv4 uses 32-bit addresses, limiting the number of unique addresses. IPv6 uses 128-bit addresses, providing a vastly larger number of possible addresses to support the growth of the internet.
